session.user:
Run fast CI checks for the erk project. Run ruff, prettier, ty, and pytest (unit tests only). Report all results.
The project is at /home/runner/work/erk/erk. Use `make` targets if available, or run the tools directly. Report results clearly.
session.assistant: I'll run the CI checks for the erk project. Let me start by examining the available make targets and then execute the checks.
session.tool_result:
1→.PHONY: format-check lint prettier prettier-check ty upgrade-ty test test-erkbot py-fast-ci fast-ci all-ci md-check docs-check docs-validate docs-sync-check docs-fix clean publish fix reinstall-erk-tools docs docs-serve docs-deploy exec-reference-check slackbot 2→ 3→prettier: 4→ prettier --write '**/*.md' --ignore-path .gitignore 5→ 6→prettier-check: 7→ prettier --check '**/*.md' --ignore-path .gitignore 8→ 9→format-check: 10→ uv run ruff format --check 11→ 12→lint: 13→ uv run ruff check 14→ 15→fix: 16→ uv run ruff check --fix --unsafe-fixes 17→ uv run ruff format 18→ prettier --write '**/*.md' --ignore-path .gitignore 19→ 20→ty: 21→ uv run ty check 22→ 23→upgrade-ty: 24→ uv remove ty --group dev && uv add --dev ty 25→ 26→# === Package-specific test targets === 27→ 28→test-erkbot: 29→ cd packages/erkbot && uv run pytest tests/ -x -q 30→
... [154 lines omitted] ...

Usage: make [options] [target] ... Options: -b, -m Ignored for compatibility. -B, --always-make Unconditionally make all targets. -C DIRECTORY, --directory=DIRECTORY Change to DIRECTORY before doing anything. -d Print lots of debugging information. --debug[=FLAGS] Print various types of debugging information. -e, --environment-overrides Environment variables override makefiles. -E STRING, --eval=STRING Evaluate STRING as a makefile statement. -f FILE, --file=FILE, --makefile=FILE Read FILE as a makefile. -h, --help Print this message and exit. -i, --ignore-errors Ignore errors from recipes. -I DIRECTORY, --include-dir=DIRECTORY Search DIRECTORY for included makefiles. -j [N], --jobs[=N] Allow N jobs at once; infinite jobs with no arg. -k, --keep-going Keep going when some targets can't be made. -l [N], --load-average[=N], --max-load[=N] Don't start multiple jobs unless load is below N. -L, --check-symlink-times Use the latest mtime between symlinks and target. -n, --just-print, --dry-run, --recon Don't actually run any recipe; just print them. -o FILE, --old-file=FILE, --assume-old=FILE Consider FILE to be very old and don't remake it. -O[TYPE], --output-sync[=TYPE] Synchronize output of parallel jobs by TYPE. -p, --print-data-base Print make's internal database. -q, --question Run no recipe; exit status says if up to date.
... [17 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Now I'll run the `py-fast-ci` target which runs ruff, prettier, ty, and unit tests without integration tests. This is the fastest CI check that covers the main linting and testing needs.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run Python fast CI checks (ruff, ty, pytest unit tests)

session.tool_result:
=== Python Fast CI ===
--- Lint --- Building erk-dev @ file:///home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-dev Downloading pygments (1.2MiB) Downloading aiohttp (1.7MiB) Downloading pydantic-core (2.0MiB) Downloading ruff (10.6MiB) Downloading libcst (2.2MiB) Downloading mkdocs-material (8.9MiB) Downloading mkdocs (3.7MiB) Downloading ty (9.6MiB) Downloading babel (9.7MiB) Downloaded aiohttp Downloaded pydantic-core Downloaded mkdocs Built erk-dev @ file:///home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-dev Downloaded ty Downloaded pygments Downloaded ruff Downloaded libcst Downloaded babel Downloaded mkdocs-material Installed 78 packages in 390ms All checks passed!
--- Format Check --- 1625 files already formatted
--- ty ---
... [111 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Excellent! All checks passed. Now let me run the prettier check separately since it wasn't included in the py-fast-ci target output I can explicitly verify:
session.tool_result: Exit code 1 Checking formatting... [[33mwarn[39m] docs/learned/integrations/github-review-decision.md [[33mwarn[39m] docs/learned/tui/status-indicators.md [[33mwarn[39m] Code style issues found in 2 files. Run Prettier with --write to fix.
session.assistant:
Perfect! I now have complete CI results. Let me provide a comprehensive summary:
## CI Check Results Summary
### Status: MOSTLY PASSED (2 prettier formatting issues)
**Overall:** 8/9 checks passed. Only prettier formatting issues found.
---
### Individual Tool Results
#### 1. **Lint (ruff check)** - PASSED - Status: All checks passed - No linting violations detected
#### 2. **Format Check (ruff format --check)** - PASSED - Status: 1625 files already formatted correctly - No formatting issues
#### 3. **Type Check (ty check)** - PASSED - Status: All checks passed - No type errors detected
#### 4. **Prettier (markdown formatting)** - FAILED - Status: Code style issues found in 2 files - Files with issues: - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/integrations/github-review-decision.md` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/status-indicators.md` - Fix: Run `prettier --write` on these files
#### 5. **Unit Tests (erk)** - PASSED - Status: 5467 tests passed - Duration: 22.70 seconds
#### 6. **Unit Tests (erk-dev)** - PASSED - Status: 136 tests passed - Duration: 4.48 seconds
#### 7. **Unit Tests (erk-statusline)** - PASSED - Status: 112 tests passed - Duration: 0.92 seconds
#### 8. **Unit Tests (erkbot)** - PASSED - Status: 114 tests passed - Duration: 2.25 seconds
**Total Unit Tests:** 5829 tests passed across all packages
---
### Summary - **Linting:** All Python code passes ruff checks - **Type checking:** All code passes ty type checks - **Python formatting:** All Python code is properly formatted - **Markdown formatting:** 2 files need prettier formatting fixes - **Tests:** All 5829 unit tests pass successfully
